# Mistake Patterns (curated)

**Read at session start when working on:** sub-agent dispatch, git/staging,
CSS/UI, error handling, frontend deploys, file refactors, comment hygiene.
Hot 5 first; top 20 below; archive (rank 21+) for the rest.

## TL;DR — top 5 (severity × recency × frequency)

1. **source-comment-hygiene** (S3, 4x since 2026-05-29) — terse, durable, no planning artifacts, no decoration

5. **adding-env-var-reads-without-checking-the-project-s-config-pattern** (S3, 2x since 2026-05-28) — Adding env var reads without checking the project's config pattern

## Clusters

- **A — Ungrounded assertion**: claim before checking. → [clusters/A](atone/derived/clusters/)
- **B — Claim-ready-before-runtime**: shipped without executing. → [clusters/B](atone/derived/clusters/)
- **C — Literal-list-as-action**: user list executed literally. → [clusters/C](atone/derived/clusters/)
- **D — Output-shape laziness**: didn't render/inspect before judging. → [clusters/D](atone/derived/clusters/)
- **E — Convention-blind code**: ignored project rules. → [clusters/E](atone/derived/clusters/)
